title: Cost Planning
description: Quiz covering cost estimating, budgeting, cost control, estimating techniques, reserves, cost baselines and earned value management.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of project cost planning?
options: To estimate, budget and control the costs required to complete the project
questions.explanation: Cost planning ensures that sufficient financial resources are available and managed throughout the project lifecycle.

questions.question: Which activity comes first in the project cost management process?
questions.explanation: Before creating a budget, the project team must first estimate the costs of the individual activities.

questions.question: What is a project cost estimate?
options: The final amount the project will definitely cost
An informed prediction of the expected cost to perform project work
A record of actual project expenses
questions.explanation: A cost estimate predicts the expected cost based on available information before the project begins.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a project budget?
options: To combine approved cost estimates into an authorized spending plan
questions.explanation: The project budget consolidates estimated costs into an approved financial plan for executing the project.

questions.question: Which statement best describes cost control?
options: Estimating the project's total cost before work begins
Monitoring actual costs and managing deviations from the approved budget
questions.explanation: Cost control involves tracking project spending and taking corrective actions when costs differ from the plan.

questions.question: A project is very similar to another completed by the company last year. Which estimating technique would typically be the most appropriate to use first?
questions.explanation: Analogous estimating uses historical information from similar completed projects to produce a quick estimate.

questions.question: Why is Bottom-Up Estimating generally more accurate than Analogous Estimating?
options: It estimates each work package individually before combining them
It always produces the lowest project cost
It only requires one previous project for comparison
questions.explanation: Bottom-Up Estimating evaluates each activity separately, resulting in greater detail and usually higher accuracy.

questions.question: Which estimating technique uses measurable relationships, such as cost per square meter or cost per kilometer, to calculate project costs?
questions.explanation: Parametric estimating applies statistical relationships between historical data and project variables.

questions.question: Three-Point Estimating improves cost estimates because it:
options: Uses only the lowest possible estimate
Considers optimistic, most likely and pessimistic scenarios
Requires the project to be completed first
questions.explanation: Three-Point Estimating accounts for uncertainty by evaluating multiple possible outcomes rather than relying on a single estimate.

questions.question: Which of the following would normally be classified as a direct project cost?
options: Concrete purchased specifically for a construction project
questions.explanation: Direct costs can be directly assigned to the project, such as project-specific materials, labor and equipment.
